title: cornus macrophylla
intro: Cornus macrophylla, also known as large-leafed dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern Asia, characterized by its large, oval-shaped leaves and showy white flowers in the spring. The bark and roots of the plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Cornus macrophylla include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
